metadata.instructions: Append new rounds to the 'rounds' array. Each round must include all fields. Bug sources: 'doc_error' = bug in the AI documentation, 'answerer_confabulation' = agent invented wrong info despite docs being correct, 'ambiguous' = unclear which caused the error. Each new round MUST include at least 1 regression question from the top-level 'regression_questions' array (added 2026-05-23); score against expected_answer_summary; if regression, set drift_observed=true and update used_in_rounds. Severity tiers + audit format: audit/flywheel_rubric.md.
metadata.root_cause_vocabulary_note: bugs_by_root_cause keys: doc_error (doc wrong AND answer wrong); doc_error_answerer_beat_it (doc wrong but answer right - LATENT, fix anyway); answerer_confabulation (doc correct, answer wrong); answerer_style_or_framing; process_gap; ambiguous. See flywheel_rubric.md sec 1.5.

regression_questions.question: Walk through how vm_carbon_stock is computed in Module 52 and where it enters the GHG-policy cost in Module 56. Cite the relevant equations and file:line locations.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: vm_carbon_stock is DECLARED in Module 56 (price_aug22/declarations.gms:34) — NOT in Module 52. Land modules (29 cropland, 31 pasture, 32 forestry, 34 urban, 35 natveg) and Module 59 (SOM, soilc pool) POPULATE the variable. Module 52 only READS vm_carbon_stock and pcm_carbon_stock to compute CO2 emissions via q52_emis_co2_actual (normal_dec17/equations.gms:16-19). Module 56's chain: q56_emis_pricing_co2 (equations.gms:19-22) reads (pcm - vm)/timestep_length → v56_emis_pricing → q56_emission_cost_oneoff (equations.gms:45-52) → v56_emission_cost → q56_emission_costs (equations.gms:56-58) → vm_emission_costs(i). The %c56_carbon_stock_pricing% switch default is 'actualNoAcEst'. Citations must use full relative paths per MANDATE 16. (Corrected 2026-05-24 R22 — earlier summary incorrectly attributed declaration to M52.)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests (a) cross-module dependency tracing, (b) precise file:line citations, (c) variable-name fidelity (no confabulation of related-sounding names like vm_carbon_stocks), (d) gate-tabulation behavior when the chain has multiple branches, (e) producer-vs-consumer distinction (which module DECLARES vs which only READS the variable)

regression_questions.question: Which version of magpie4 does this agent's source-of-truth clone reflect, and how was that version determined? Cite the file(s) you read.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: Agent must read project/version_pins.json (NOT the workspace clone at ~/Documents/Work/Workspace/magpie4/). Reports the version and SHA from that file; identifies the upstream authoritative source as ../input/renv.lock (Packages.magpie4 entry — fields: Version + RemoteSha). Verification at audit time: read project/version_pins.json directly — the agent's claimed version, SHA (or 10-char prefix), and 'resolution' field must match. The workspace clone is intentionally NOT the source-of-truth (it drifts ahead of the renv pin; as of 2026-05-24 it is v2.75.1 while the renv pin is v2.70.0). The pin can be regenerated via 'python3 scripts/sync_magpie4_clone.py'. Critical-severity confabulation: claiming a different version than version_pins.json shows; citing the workspace clone as source-of-truth; fabricating a SHA. Citations must use full relative paths per MANDATE 16. (Added 2026-05-24 alongside the magpie4 lean-scaffolding initiative.)
ground_truth_sources: project/version_pins.json (cached snapshot of the pin; regenerated by scripts/sync_magpie4_clone.py)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests (a) source-of-truth discipline for the two-clone setup (SHA-pinned .cache/sources/magpie4/ vs drift-prone ~/Documents/Work/Workspace/magpie4/), (b) reading the auto-loaded agent/helpers/magpie4_reference.md helper before answering (the three pre-answer rules), (c) version-pin awareness (not confabulating a version from training data)

regression_questions.question: How does magpie4::getReport organize its reporting? Describe the dispatch pattern, how many unique report* functions it calls, and cite the file:line range from the pinned clone.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: Agent must read from .cache/sources/magpie4/R/getReport.R (the SHA-pinned clone), NOT from the workspace clone. Dispatch is a flat tryList(...) of unconditional calls to ~106 unique report* functions (117 total call lines — some functions called multiple times with different argument combos, e.g., reportYields with physical=TRUE/FALSE, reportProcessing with two indicator values, reportPriceFoodIndex with two baseyears). NOT an 'if (any(grepl(...)))' or control-argument dispatch — there is NO 'control' argument; the function signature is getReport(gdx, file=NULL, scenario=NULL, filter=c(1,2,7), detail=TRUE, level='regglo', ...). tryList catches per-function failures so one broken report* does not crash the whole report. Body lives at .cache/sources/magpie4/R/getReport.R lines 56-235 (v2.70.0 @ a360d8c9ec); the tryList block is approximately lines 62-181. Major-severity bug to catch: confabulating a control-arg-gated dispatch (this was the magpie4 scaffolding plan's incorrect initial description; the helper documents the corrected structure). Critical if the agent claims a different unique-function count by an order of magnitude (e.g., 'thousands' or '20'). (Added 2026-05-24 alongside the magpie4 lean-scaffolding initiative.)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests (a) reading from the SHA-pinned clone rather than the workspace clone, (b) faithful description of the actual dispatch (no confabulation of control-based gating or grepl filtering), (c) reasonable count of unique report* functions (target ~106; off-by-few is Minor, off-by-order is Critical), (d) precise file:line citation with version pin per MANDATE 16
